docker_help_entries.title: Start All Services
docker_help_entries.description: Start cycleCAD, converter, signaling, and optional ExplodeView
docker_help_entries.example: Start services in background and check status

docker_help_entries.title: Stop All Services
docker_help_entries.description: Stop running services while preserving data

docker_help_entries.title: View Service Logs
docker_help_entries.example: View all logs in real-time, or filter by service
docker_help_entries.output: Timestamped log entries from selected service(s)

docker_help_entries.title: Check Service Status
docker_help_entries.description: View running status and health checks
docker_help_entries.example: Show health status and resource usage

docker_help_entries.title: Execute Commands in Service
docker_help_entries.example: Open shell or run single command in service

docker_help_entries.title: Build or Rebuild Images
docker_help_entries.example: Full rebuild (all services) or specific service

docker_help_entries.title: Test STEP Conversion
docker_help_entries.description: Upload STEP file and get GLB output
docker_help_entries.example: Convert a STEP file to GLB format
docker_help_entries.output: Binary GLB file (3D model in glTF format)

docker_help_entries.title: Converter API Documentation
docker_help_entries.example: View all converter endpoints and test them

docker_help_entries.title: Restart Services
docker_help_entries.description: Restart running services (useful after config changes)
docker_help_entries.example: Restart all services or specific one

docker_help_entries.title: Manage Volumes
docker_help_entries.description: View and manage Docker volumes (persistent data)
docker_help_entries.example: List all volumes, inspect one, or delete

docker_help_entries.title: Clean Up Unused Resources
docker_help_entries.description: Remove dangling images, containers, and volumes
docker_help_entries.example: Free up disk space by removing unused resources

docker_help_entries.title: Persistent Logging
docker_help_entries.description: Save logs to file and view later
docker_help_entries.example: Redirect logs to file for debugging
docker_help_entries.output: Log file created with full output

tips.tip: Use 'docker-compose up -d' in production, 'docker-compose up' in development to see logs
Always run health checks after starting: 'docker-compose ps' should show (healthy)
Set LOG_LEVEL=debug in .env for troubleshooting, use LOG_LEVEL=warn in production
Use 'docker-compose logs --since 30m' to view logs from last 30 minutes
Scale converter with multiple instances for heavy STEP processing: add converter-2, converter-3 in docker-compose.yml
